title: Intensely Briny Japanese Salmon Onigiri
description: A briny, shaggy, meal-prep Japanese dish featuring marjoram and salmon, wrapped in corn husks for a unique flavor and texture, this dish is kosher-friendly and perfect for a snack or lunch
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit
Season the salmon fillets with kosher salt and black pepper
Bake the salmon in the oven for 12 minutes or until cooked through
Allow the salmon to rest for 5 minutes before flaking it into small pieces
Mix the cooked Japanese rice with rice vinegar and water in a large bowl
Add the flaked salmon and chopped marjoram to the rice mixture and stir until combined
Lay a corn husk flat on a work surface and place a small amount of the salmon and rice mixture in the center of the husk
Fold the corn husk over the filling to form a triangle and repeat with the remaining ingredients
Serve the onigiri at room temperature or chilled
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days
